title: Iterators 3
description: The `map()` adapter transforms each element of an iterator using a closure.
instructions: Use the map iterator adapter to transform a collection.
hints: The map method takes a closure: .map(|x| x * 2)
Since iter() gives references, you may need to dereference: .map(|&x| x * 2)
explanation: The `map()` method creates a new iterator that transforms each element using the provided closure. It is lazy - it doesn't do anything until consumed (e.g., by `collect()`). You can destructure the reference in the closure parameter (`|&x|`) or dereference inside the closure (`|x| *x * 2`). Chaining map with collect is a very common Rust pattern.
